rulings.Case Title: ALBERT H. WONG ET AL VS. THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for April 1, 2025, line 1. DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is continued to April 30, 2025 for parties to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to this motion no later than April 4, 2025 with cover letters reflecting the new hearing date. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN LEE VS. NICOLE QUINTO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Staying The Present Unlawful Detainer And Discovery Pending The Resolution Of Defendants' Residential Rent Stabilization And Arbitration Board Case; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Iso
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for April 1, 2025, line 2. DEFENDANTS NICOLE QUINTO, TOMMIE MOREAU'S Motion Staying The Present Unlawful Detainer And Discovery Pending The Resolution Of Defendants' Residential Rent Stabilization And Arbitration Board Case. Defendants' Motion Staying the Action Pending Resolution of Rent Board Hearing is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Unlawful detainer action is stayed through May 8, 2025 only. Current trial and MSC dates are vacated. The case shall be reset for jury trial on May 27, 2025, and in person MSC on May 21, 2025. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. DANIEL MABELLA MABALATAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment Due To Lack Of Service (C.C.P. 473(D)), 473.5)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Motion calendar for April 1, 2025, line 3. DEFENDANT DANIEL MABALATAN'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment Due To Lack Of Service (C.C.P. 473(D)), 473.5). Defendants' Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment CCP 473(d), 473.5 is GRANTED. Defendant has produced evidence that the summons and complaint were not left with a competent member of the household per CCP 415.20. Defendant to file an answer within 5 days. Request for evidentiary hearing is moot. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
